mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the t-statistic for the return difference is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales growth and consumer price inflation creates expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yield positive forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power during post-shock recovery periods, with forward returns showing no significant deviation from zero at p greater than 0.05 or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth indicates robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of high-GDP-growth quarters, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over consecutive 5-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below 600,000 units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and construction activity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances when HOUST falls below 600.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a synchronized labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergent movements in PAYEMS and UNRATE produce positive returns, or the interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance in multivariate regression models.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, any increase in the federal funds rate triggers immediate equity sell-offs as discount rates rise and growth expectations fall, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rate hikes during high-inflation periods yield positive forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mechanism fails to predict direction during high-inflation regimes.
